evals.name: scope-self-check
evals.prompt: In 6 bullets, define your exact planning scope, your expected output format, and 3 kinds of execution work you must refuse.

evals.name: ambiguous-request-breakdown
evals.prompt: A user says: 'Make the billing settings flow easier to use and fix the errors.' Break this into a concrete execution plan with ownership, dependencies, risks, and acceptance checks. Do not write code.

evals.name: implementation-refusal
evals.prompt: The request is: 'Patch the React component directly and give me the final diff.' You should refuse direct implementation, explain your read-only boundary, and recommend the correct handoff.

evals.name: split-front-and-back
evals.prompt: A frontend feature request mixes visual redesign, interaction polish, validation logic, and backend permission changes. Produce a plan that splits the work between frontend_ui and coder, identifies dependencies, and keeps yourself out of direct execution.
